text.title: Cloud Run Deployment Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every application of `Cloud Run Deployment` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every deploy MUST create a tagged revision (`--tag <semver>`); untagged deploys forbidden because they cannot be referenced for rollback.
rule.rationale: Untagged revisions cannot be addressed by URL; tags make canary + rollback addressable.

rule.statement: Production deploys MUST go via traffic split (10% → 50% → 100% over ≥ 15 min) for non-trivial changes; instant 100% allowed only for hotfixes with named approver.

rationale.source: Canary deployment best practices; GCP deployment guides

rule.rationale: Instant 100% is high risk; canary catches regressions on a fraction of traffic before full rollout.

rule.statement: Cloud Run service MUST have a dedicated service account with least-privilege IAM; default Compute Engine service account is forbidden.

rationale.source: GCP IAM Best Practices; CIS GCP Benchmark

rule.rationale: Default Compute SA has broad permissions; per-service SAs bound blast radius.

rule.statement: Secrets MUST come from Secret Manager mounts (env var or volume); plain-text env vars with credentials forbidden.

rationale.source: Cloud Run Secret Manager integration docs

rule.rationale: Env vars are visible in console + logs + revision YAML; Secret Manager keeps secrets out of revision metadata.

rule.statement: Container images MUST come from Artifact Registry (not Docker Hub / public registries) and MUST be pinned by digest (sha256:..), not by tag.

rationale.source: Cloud Run + Artifact Registry docs; supply-chain best practices

rule.rationale: Tag-pinned images drift; mutable upstreams have been compromised (SolarWinds-style); digest pinning is reproducible.

rule.statement: Ingress MUST be set per service: `internal` for internal services, `internal-and-cloud-load-balancing` for fronted services, `all` only for explicitly public services.
rule.rationale: Default `all` exposes private services publicly; explicit ingress is the defensible baseline.

rule.statement: Deploys MUST go through Cloud Build / GitHub Actions with a named approval gate for production. Manual `gcloud run deploy` from a developer machine forbidden for prod.
rule.rationale: Manual deploys skip review + audit trail; CI-with-approval gates make every deploy reviewable.

rule.statement: Cloud Run logs MUST be JSON-structured with trace + span IDs; Cloud Trace + Cloud Profiler MUST be enabled for production services.
rule.rationale: Without structured logs + tracing, cross-service debugging falls back to grep; observability is the auditor surface.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
